title: SQL Lab 10: Top 10 Percent Customers by Spend
short_description: Identify customers in the top spending percentile band.
business_context: Retention and loyalty programs often target the highest-spend customers using percentile-based segmentation.
problem_statement: Return customers in the top 10 percent by total_spend.
student_task: Write a query returning customer_id and total_spend.
learning_goals: Use percent ranking for percentile thresholds
Understand ascending versus descending percentile logic
Avoid arbitrary LIMIT when the requirement is percentile-based
broken_code:
SELECT customer_id, total_spend
FROM customer_spend
ORDER BY total_spend DESC
LIMIT 10;
production_logs: loyalty-job: small cohort table returned too many customers
review-note: top 10 rows is not the same as top 10 percent
solution_query:
WITH ranked AS (
  SELECT customer_id,
         total_spend,
         PERCENT_RANK() OVER (ORDER BY total_spend) AS pr
  FROM customer_spend
)
SELECT customer_id, total_spend
FROM ranked
WHERE pr >= 0.9;
explanation: PERCENT_RANK over ascending spend gives the highest spender a percentile rank of 1. Filtering at 0.9 returns the top percentile band.
common_mistakes: Using LIMIT 10 regardless of table size
Ordering percent rank in the wrong direction
Not considering ties near the threshold
hints: With ascending order, high spend has high percent rank.
